org.apache.paimon.flink.action.cdc.mongodb

Class MongoDBSyncDatabaseAction

  • java.lang.Object
    • org.apache.paimon.flink.action.ActionBase
      • org.apache.paimon.flink.action.cdc.mongodb.MongoDBSyncDatabaseAction
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    Action

    public class MongoDBSyncDatabaseAction
extends ActionBase
    An action class responsible for synchronizing MongoDB databases with a target system.

    This class provides functionality to read data from a MongoDB source, process it, and then synchronize it with a target system. It supports various configurations, including table prefixes, suffixes, and inclusion/exclusion patterns.

    Key features include:

    • Support for case-sensitive and case-insensitive database and table names.
    • Configurable table name conversion with prefixes and suffixes.
    • Ability to include or exclude specific tables using regular expressions.
    • Integration with Flink's streaming environment for data processing.

    Note: This action is primarily intended for use in Flink streaming applications that synchronize MongoDB data with other systems.

      • build

        public void build()
           throws Exception
        Description copied from interface: Action
        Builds the action within the given Flink Stream Execution Environment.

        This method is responsible for setting up any necessary configurations or resources needed for the action to run. It is called before the `run` method to prepare the environment for execution.

        By default, this method is empty and can be overridden by subclasses to provide custom setup logic.

        Throws:
        Exception - If an error occurs during the build process.

      • tableConfig

        @VisibleForTesting
public Map<String,String> tableConfig()

      • run

        public void run()
         throws Exception
        Description copied from interface: Action
        The execution method of the action.
        Throws:
        Exception
